output:
1	---
     2	name: tavily-map
     3	description: |
     4	  Discover and list all URLs on a website without extracting content, via the Tavily CLI. Use this skill when the user wants to find a specific page on a large site, list all URLs, see the site structure, find where something is on a domain, or says "map the site", "find the URL for", "what pages are on", "list all pages", or "site structure". Faster than crawling — returns URLs only. Essential when you know the site but not the exact page. Combine with extract for targeted content retrieval.
     5	allowed-tools: Bash(tvly *)
     6	---
     7	
     8	# tavily map
     9	
    10	Discover URLs on a website without extracting content. Faster than crawling.
    11	
    12	## Before running any command
    13	
    14	If `tvly` is not found on PATH, install it first:
    15	
    16	```bash
    17	curl -fsSL https://cli.tavily.com/install.sh | bash && tvly login
    18	```
    19	
    20	Do not skip this step or fall back to other tools.
    21	
    22	See [tavily-cli](../tavily-cli/SKILL.md) for alternative install methods and auth options.
    23	
    24	## When to use
    25	
    26	- You need to find a specific subpage on a large site
    27	- You want a list of all URLs before deciding what to extract or crawl
    28	- Step 3 in the [workflow](../tavily-cli/SKILL.md): search → extract → **map** → crawl → research
    29	
    30	## Quick start
    31	
    32	```bash
    33	# Discover all URLs
    34	tvly map "https://docs.example.com" --json
    35	
    36	# With natural language filtering
    37	tvly map "https://docs.example.com" --instructions "Find API docs and guides" --json
    38	
    39	# Filter by path
    40	tvly map "https://example.com" --select-paths "/blog/.*" --limit 500 --json
    41	
    42	# Deep map
    43	tvly map "https://example.com" --max-depth 3 --limit 200 --json
    44	```
    45	
    46	## Options
    47	
    48	| Option | Description |
    49	|--------|-------------|
    50	| `--max-depth` | Levels deep (1-5, default: 1) |
    51	| `--max-breadth` | Links per page (default: 20) |
    52	| `--limit` | Max URLs to discover (default: 50) |
    53	| `--instructions` | Natural language guidance for URL filtering |
    54	| `--select-paths` | Comma-separated regex patterns to include |
    55	| `--exclude-paths` | Comma-separated regex patterns to exclude |
    56	| `--select-domains` | Comma-separated regex for domains to include |
    57	| `--exclude-domains` | Comma-separated regex for domains to exclude |
    58	| `--allow-external / --no-external` | Include external links |
    59	| `--timeout` | Max wait (10-150 seconds) |
    60	| `-o, --output` | Save output to file |
    61	| `--json` | Structured JSON output |
    62	
    63	## Map + Extract pattern
    64	
    65	Use `map` to find the right page, then `extract` it. This is often more efficient than crawling an entire site:
    66	
    67	```bash
    68	# Step 1: Find the authentication docs
    69	tvly map "https://docs.example.com" --instructions "authentication" --json
    70	
    71	# Step 2: Extract the specific page you found
    72	tvly extract "https://docs.example.com/api/authentication" --json
    73	```
    74	
    75	## Tips
    76	
    77	- **Map is URL discovery only** — no content extraction. Use `extract` or `crawl` for content.
    78	- **Map + extract beats crawl** when you only need a few specific pages from a large site.
    79	- **Use `--instructions`** for semantic filtering when path patterns aren't enough.
    80	
    81	## See also
    82	
    83	- [tavily-extract](../tavily-extract/SKILL.md) — extract content from URLs you discover
    84	- [tavily-crawl](../tavily-crawl/SKILL.md) — bulk extract when you need many pages
